model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Purdue’s elite offense is the most bankable unit in this matchup (top-2 KenPom AdjO; #1 Torvik AdjO), and Gonzaga’s path gets tougher if Braden Huff remains sidelined, as recent reporting suggests he’s unlikely to be back yet. Gonzaga’s top-10 defense gives them a very credible upset route if they can turn it into a lower-possession grind and keep Purdue off the foul line/second chances, but Purdue’s shot-quality creation and overall efficiency edge make them the slightly safer Sweet 16 pick.

reasoningStep.title: West Sweet 16 2 — Gonzaga vs Purdue
reasoningStep.summary: Lean Purdue due to a clear offensive advantage and Gonzaga’s apparent ongoing frontcourt availability concern (Huff).
evidence: KenPom: Purdue #8 (AdjEM 30.5; AdjO 131.0 #2; AdjD 100.5) vs Gonzaga #11 (AdjEM 28.16; AdjO 122.1 #30; AdjD 93.9).
Torvik: Purdue #8 (AdjO 132.56 #1; AdjD 100.23 #36) vs Gonzaga #11 (AdjO 120.26 #37; AdjD 93.97 #9).
Injury/news context: multiple reports indicate Gonzaga has been without Braden Huff since mid-January and suggest he’s unlikely to return yet, reducing Gonzaga’s frontcourt margin against Purdue.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Cole Forsman | Share on Facebook Share on X Add us as a preferred source Gonzaga Bulldogs foward Braden Huff. | Photo by Myk Crawford In this story: Gonzaga Bulldogs The Gonzaga men's basketball team has advanced to the second weekend of the NCAA Tournament nine times since 2014. --> The Gonzaga men's basketball team has advanced to the second weekend of the NCAA Tournament nine times since 2014. If the Bulldogs are to make it 10 Sweet 16 appearances in the past dozen years, it sounds like they'll have to do without one of their best players. --> If the Bulldogs are to make it 10 Sweet 16 appearances in the past dozen years, it sounds like they'll have to do without one of their best players. After his team heard its name called during the Selection Sunday show for the 2026 NCAA Tournament, Mark Few was less than optimistic about Huff's chances of playing in the first and — if Gonzaga gets by Kennesaw State — second rounds. --> After his team heard its name called during the Selection Sunday show for the 2026 NCAA Tournament, Mark Few was less than optimistic about Huff's chances of playing in the first and — if Gonzaga gets by Kennesaw State — second rounds. "I don't think he's gonna be able to play this weekend or anything," Few said. "But he is jogging and shooting." --> "I don't think he's gonna be able to play this weekend or anything," Few said. "But he is jogging and shooting." Huff has not played since suffering a knee injury ahead of Gonzaga's road game at Washington State on Jan. 15. The team initially gave a 4-8 week timeline for his return, with the tail end of that window coming right at the start of the NCAA Tournament. --> Huff has not played since suffering a knee injury ahead of Gonzaga's road game at Washington State on Jan. 15. The team initially gave a 4-8 week timeline for his return, with the tail end of that window coming right at the start of the NCAA Tournament. Huff was not made available for comment on Selection Sunday. The 6-foot-10 redshirt junior was seen helping his teammates warm up for their West Coast Conference semifinal game against Oregon State in Las Vegas last week, notably without crutches at his side. --> Huff was not made available for comment on Selection Sunday. Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. But Huff’s absence affects the Zags’ future, and his doubt creates a bittersweet aftertaste during this time when celebration is the mode of the moment. Bitter? Obvious. Wondering what t

Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
